options: Employee meal during business travel (travel expense)
Employee meal working late (employee benefits/meals expense)
Meeting meal with business partner (meals and entertainment expense)
Office meal for team meeting (office expense or employee benefits)
ambiguities.clarification_question: What was the business purpose of this $125 meal?
ambiguities.why_entry_differs: Different meal purposes map to different expense accounts (e.g., meals & entertainment vs. travel expense vs. employee benefits), which affects both classification and tax treatment (50% deductibility rules may apply differently)
ambiguities.why_not_resolved: No standard convention dictates how to classify meal expenses without knowing the business context. The transaction states only 'a meal' without indicating whether it was for clients, travel, employee benefits, or another purpose. Business type 'general' and corporate structure do not narrow the interpretation.
